title: Screw Gravity
description: Vanilla-friendly pack based around Gravity Changer with QoL mods.
body:
## Want to play with gravity? This is your starting point
_A simple starting point for playing with gravity._

Dive into a whole new dimension of gameplay with Screw Gravity,
a vanilla-friendly modpack centered around the Gravity Changer mod!
Experience Minecraft like never before by flipping the world on its head—literally.
With Gravity Changer, you have full control over the direction of gravity,
allowing you to walk on walls, ceilings, and explore from mind-bending perspectives.

Designed to stay close to the vanilla feel, Screw Gravity enhances your gameplay with quality-of-life improvements and mods that complement this gravity-defying experience.
Whether you're looking to build in impossible ways or tackle Minecraft’s challenges from new angles, this modpack keeps the core spirit of the game intact while turning your adventure upside down (or sideways)!

![Image of a player with sideways gravity next to a horizontal door](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/6bbc426e811a00cdb13591143ef8e40ad3109512.png)
### Crafting
You can now make vertical slabs, stairs, and doors!
You can convert them to and from their vertical variants on the crafting table.

### Commands
* `/gravity set_base_direction <direction> [entities]` sets the base gravity direction.
* `/gravity set_base_strength <strength> [entities]` sets the base gravity strength.
* `/gravity view` shows the command sender's base gravity direction and strength.
* `/gravity reset [entities]` reset the base gravity direction and strength.
* `/gravity randomize_base_direction [entities]` sets the base direction as random.
* `/gravity set_relative_base_direction <relativeDirection> [entities]` sets the gravity direction relative to the entity's viewing direction. The `<relativeDirection>` can be `forward`, `backward`, `left`, `right`, `up`, or `down`.
* `/gravity set_dimension_gravity_strength <strength>` sets the dimensional gravity strength for the current dimension.
* `/gravity view_dimension_info` shows the dimensional gravity strength for the current dimension.
